perturbation.original_text: Affiliate is only eligible to earn the Advertising Cost Compensation on sales occurring during the term of this Agreement and fees earned up to the date termination will remain payable only if the related orders are not cancelled or returned.
perturbation.changed_text: Affiliate is only eligible to earn the Advertising Cost Compensation on sales occurring during the term of this Agreement. All fees earned up to the date of termination are non-payable, regardless of order status.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original statement by specifying that fees earned up to the termination date are not payable under any circumstances, while the original clause conditioned payability on whether the related orders were cancelled or returned. It introduces an in-text contradiction because now one section says fees *may* be payable after termination under certain conditions, while this altered section states they are *never* payable. This creates ambiguity around the affiliate's final compensation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: element 5 will pay Affiliate its advertising costs (including Vat, if any, on presentation of a valid VAT invoice) if an end user arrives via the link integrated in Affiliate's Web site at the order page hosted by element 5 for the respective Software Source: SOUTHERN STAR ENERGY INC., SB-2/A, 12/2/2005 Publisher's Product under this Agreement and end user uses the automatic ordering system that carries out the registration and payment processing, so that the Affiliate's link is deemed to be directly causative for the entry of the End User into the agreement with the Software Publisher and End User's payment, proving that all requirements are irrevocably met ("agreements arising in a qualified manner"). Affiliate is only eligible to earn the Advertising Cost Compensation on sales occurring during the term of this Agreement. All fees earned up to the date of termination are non-payable, regardless of order status. (2) Insofar as the referred end user accepts cookies when clicking on Affiliate's link, proceeds from orders will be taken into account which do arise within the lifespan (of max. 180 days) of the cookie(s) used and/ or possibly within the lifespan of a special action of the Software Publisher via integrated link.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that 'All fees earned up to the date of termination are non-payable, regardless of order status.' This directly contradicts the original text's implied condition of payability if 'the related orders are not cancelled or returned.' Since the changed text removes this condition and makes all fees non-payable, it is an in-text contradiction according to the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' because it creates uncertainty and ambiguity around final compensation, making the enforcement of payment terms unclear.

perturbation.original_text: The Affiliate should be aware that the product prices and availability may change at any time. It is not recommended that the price be listed on the Web site of the Affiliate. Maintenance of these product prices is possible only on the Web sites of the Software Publisher and the Publisher's corresponding site operated by element 5.
perturbation.changed_text: The Affiliate is required to display the most current and accurate product prices and availability at all times. Affiliate will update its webpage at least once per week or will be subject to a penalty fee that may be deducted from its revenues. Maintenance of these product prices is not possible on the Web sites of the Software Publisher and the Publisher's corresponding site operated by element 5.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text introduces a contradiction by mandating the Affiliate to display product prices and maintain their accuracy, directly conflicting with the original recommendation against listing prices due to potential changes and the statement that maintaining the prices is possible only on the software publisher's website. The requirement and the penalty for non-compliance now impose a duty on the affiliate that is impossible to satisfy given the statement that maintenance of these product prices is not possible on the web sites of the Software Publisher. This creates a contractual ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (3) The Affiliate shall produce a brief description for each activated product on its Web site. The Affiliate is responsible for the content, the style and the layout of this information. The Software Publisher can provide the Affiliate with graphics and text in an electronic format for use in describing the product, which the Affiliate shall use for the purpose of advertising the Products under the Agreement and links according to the instructions of the Software Publisher. The Affiliate is required to display the most current and accurate product prices and availability at all times. Affiliate will update its webpage at least once per week or will be subject to a penalty fee that may be deducted from its revenues. Maintenance of these product prices is not possible on the Web sites of the Software Publisher and the Publisher's corresponding site operated by element 5.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. It first states, 'The Affiliate is required to display the most current and accurate product prices and availability at all times' and then states 'Maintenance of these product prices is not possible on the Web sites of the Software Publisher'. This creates an impossible obligation on the Affiliate, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction and a contractual ambiguity. This satisfies the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may cancel this Agreement at any time, with or without supplying a reason, through written notification or by making suitable settings in the respective Control Panel.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may cancel this Agreement only by providing a 30-day written notification specifying the reason for cancellation. Termination through the Control Panel is not permitted.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction regarding the termination clause. Originally, either party could cancel 'at any time, with or without supplying a reason' through written notification or the Control Panel. The changed text now requires a 30-day written notice *with* a reason and explicitly prohibits termination via the Control Panel. If the original text is still assumed to be in effect, there is now an in-text contradiction and uncertainty as to how the contract should be terminated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement will begin upon acceptance of Affiliate's Program application and will end when terminated by either party of this Agreement. This Agreement is entered into for an unlimited period of time. Either party may cancel this Agreement only by providing a 30-day written notification specifying the reason for cancellation. Termination through the Control Panel is not permitted.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text allowed cancellation 'at any time, with or without supplying a reason' and permitted termination through the Control Panel. The changed text mandates a 30-day written notice 'with a reason' and forbids termination via the Control Panel. This creates a direct conflict within the agreement regarding the proper termination procedure and meets the definition of an in-text contradiction as specified in Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.
